name: Three-way quicksort for arrays with duplicates

variants.task: Create /tmp/eval_qs/qs.py — reads from stdin: line 1 is T (test case count). For each case, line A is N >= 1 and line B is N space-separated integers. For each case, output the sorted integers as space-separated values on their own line. Implement three-way quicksort yourself (split into <pivot, ==pivot, >pivot regions, must handle duplicates efficiently). Do NOT call sorted() or list.sort() on the input data.

validation.script: set -e; cd /tmp/eval_qs; ! grep -qE '\bsorted\s*\(|\.sort\s*\(' qs.py || { echo 'must not call sorted() or .sort()'; exit 1; }; python3 qs.py < input.txt > out.txt; diff -u expected.txt out.txt && echo OK

variants.task: Create /tmp/eval_qs/qs.go (package main). Reads from stdin: line 1 is T, then per case line A is N and line B is N space-separated integers. Output each sorted array on its own line. Implement three-way quicksort yourself — do NOT use sort.Ints, sort.Slice, sort.Sort, or any std sort. Compile with `go build -o qs qs.go`.

validation.script: set -e; cd /tmp/eval_qs; ! grep -qE 'sort\.(Ints|Slice|Sort|Strings|SliceStable|IntSlice)' qs.go || { echo 'must not use std sort'; exit 1; }; go build -o qs qs.go; ./qs < input.txt > out.txt; diff -u expected.txt out.txt && echo OK

variants.task: Create /tmp/eval_qs/qs.c — reads from stdin: line 1 is T, then per case line A is N and line B is N space-separated integers. Output each sorted array on its own line. Implement three-way quicksort yourself — do NOT call qsort. Must compile cleanly with `gcc -O2 -std=c11 -Wall -Wextra -o qs qs.c`.

validation.script: set -e; cd /tmp/eval_qs; ! grep -qE '\bqsort\s*\(' qs.c || { echo 'must not call qsort'; exit 1; }; gcc -O2 -std=c11 -Wall -Wextra -o qs qs.c; ./qs < input.txt > out.txt; diff -u expected.txt out.txt && echo OK

variants.task: Create /tmp/eval_qs/qs.cpp — reads from stdin: line 1 is T, then per case line A is N and line B is N space-separated integers. Output each sorted array on its own line. Implement three-way quicksort yourself — do NOT call std::sort, std::stable_sort, std::partial_sort, std::nth_element, or qsort. Must compile cleanly with `g++ -O2 -std=c++17 -Wall -Wextra -o qs qs.cpp`.

validation.script: set -e; cd /tmp/eval_qs; ! grep -qE 'std::(sort|stable_sort|partial_sort|nth_element)\b|\bqsort\s*\(' qs.cpp || { echo 'must not call std sort'; exit 1; }; g++ -O2 -std=c++17 -Wall -Wextra -o qs qs.cpp; ./qs < input.txt > out.txt; diff -u expected.txt out.txt && echo OK

variants.task: Create /tmp/eval_qs/qs.rs — same I/O contract as the other variants (see Python/Go/C/C++ siblings). Idiomatic Rust 2021+. Will be compiled with `rustc -O qs.rs -o qs` and tested by piping `input.txt` through stdin and checking against `expected.txt` (or via a check.py validator).

validation.script: set -e; cd /tmp/eval_qs && rustc -O qs.rs -o qs && ./qs < input.txt > out.txt && diff -u expected.txt out.txt && echo OK

variants.task: Create /tmp/eval_qs/qs.zig — same I/O contract as the other variants. Use Zig 0.16+ idioms. Entrypoint: `pub fn main(init: std.process.Init) !void`. For stdio, the rich API (`takeDelimiter`, `appendRemainingUnlimited`, `print`, `writeAll`, `flush`) lives on the `std.Io.Reader`/`Writer` *interface*, NOT on the concrete `std.Io.File.Reader`/`Writer`. Reach the interface via `&fr.interface`. Canonical setup: `var in_buf: [4096]u8 = undefined; var fr = std.Io.File.stdin().reader(init.io, &in_buf); const r = &fr.interface;` and the symmetric writer. Then `try r.takeDelimiter('\n')` (returns `?[]u8`, null at EOF) for line-by-line, or `try r.appendRemainingUnlimited(init.arena.allocator(), &list)` to slurp. Always `try w.flush()` before return — output is buffered. Strict-default footguns (errors, not warnings, in 0.16): rename unused params to `_`, discard unused locals with `_ = x;`, prefer `const` over `var` when not mutated. Use `std.Io` (capital I), not `std.io`. Will be compiled with `zig build-exe -O ReleaseFast qs.zig` and tested by piping `input.txt` through stdin and checking against `expected.txt` (or via a check.py validator).

validation.script: set -e; cd /tmp/eval_qs && zig build-exe -O ReleaseFast qs.zig && ./qs < input.txt > out.txt && diff -u expected.txt out.txt && echo OK
